title: Container and BoxDecoration
description: Styling with colors, borders, and shadows.

slides.title: Container üé®
slides.content:
# Container

`Container` is a versatile widget that combines painting, positioning, and sizing.

Common properties:
* `color` ‚Äì background color.
* `padding`, `margin` ‚Äì space inside/outside.
* `decoration` ‚Äì a `BoxDecoration` for borders, gradients, shadows.
* `width`, `height` ‚Äì fixed dimensions.
* `alignment` ‚Äì align the child inside.

quizData.question: Which property of Container would you use to add a border?
quizData.explanation: You provide a BoxDecoration to the decoration property to add borders, gradients, etc.

slides.title: Container with BoxDecoration
slides.codeSnippet:
Container(
  width: 200,
  height: 200,
  decoration: BoxDecoration(
    color: Colors.blue,
    borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(16),
    boxShadow: [
      BoxShadow(
        color: Colors.grey.withOpacity(0.5),
        spreadRadius: 5,
        blurRadius: 7,
        offset: Offset(0, 3),
      ),
    ],
    border: Border.all(color: Colors.black, width: 2),
  ),
  child: Center(child: Text('Hello')),
);

slides.title: Pro Tip üöÄ
slides.content:
## Don't set both color and decoration.color

If you set both `color` and `decoration`, the color will be ignored. Use only `decoration` when you need more than a solid color.
